strict_unit_definition: A unique named organization with public evidence that its operating system learns or improves through a documented feedback, evaluation, memory, experimentation, decision, failure-learning, specialized-agent, workflow-adaptation, knowledge-curation, or governance loop. A mere AI deployment, tool listing, hypothetical use case, theory paper, or vendor claim without an organization-level improvement loop does not qualify.
counting_rule: Strict counts include only entries individually shown to meet strict_unit_definition. Zero means no qualifying entry was proven from the retrieved public evidence; it does not assert that none exists behind an inaccessible interface or after a future full audit.

largest_claim.reason: No exhaustive public-corpus universe or row-level like-for-like audit has been established. Large adjacent collections use different units, and the closest repository does not expose organization case records.

fallbacks: GitHub public API via authenticated gh CLI

collections.name: Evidently ML and LLM system design case-study database
collections.scope: Practical ML, LLM, and generative-AI product/application case studies from 150+ companies.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-stated exact count on landing page
collections.strict_count_method: Landing-page scope and schema audit only; the 800 rows are application/system designs, not organization-level learning-loop records, and no row-level strict audit was available in the retrieved page.
exclusions: ML/LLM product implementations without an organizational improvement loop
multiple use cases from one company

retrieval_evidence.evidence: The page states 800 case studies from 150+ companies, first published 2023-06-14 and last updated 2025-12-22; its filters describe industries, business/ML use cases, content types, and technology tags.

collections.name: Enterprise AI Case Studies
collections.scope: Human-curated reference library of documented enterprise AI deployments with architecture, implementation, evaluation, and citations.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-stated exact count on landing page, cross-checked against 67 case links in the retrieved rendering
collections.strict_count_method: All 67 landing-page records were title/summary screened. They document AI use cases or operational systems, but the index does not establish a named organization's organizational learning/improvement loop as the unit of record.
exclusions: self-healing technical infrastructure without evidence the organization itself learns

retrieval_evidence.method: Jina Reader public landing and about pages
retrieval_evidence.evidence: Landing page states 67 Published and 8 Categories. About page specifies named organizations, production systems/pilots, verifiable metrics, and a five-document structure; individual pages carry Updated dates but the collection has no stated global update date.

collections.name: Case Ledger
collections.scope: Claimed searchable AI use-case and ROI ledger spanning industries, functions, workflows, maturity, sources, and failure variants.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-stated exact homepage counter; not independently enumerable from the retrieved public page
collections.strict_count_method: Homepage and example-record audit only. The public evidence did not expose an enumerable row set or prove any record whose unit is a named self-learning organization.
exclusions: ROI records lacking a named organization-level learning loop

retrieval_evidence.evidence: Homepage displayed Verified Cases 4,217, Source Docs 62k+, and an example CL-4188 with sector, baseline, ROI and sources; browse-row enumeration was not present in the retrieved rendering.

collections.name: CaseStudies.com
collections.scope: B2B vendor/customer success-story directory across software, healthcare, manufacturing, fintech, and other industries.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-stated lower-bound counter (928,650+) on directory page; stored numeric value is the advertised floor
collections.strict_count_method: Directory-scope audit only. Its unit is a vendor/customer story and no strict topical filter or row-level organizational-loop audit was available.

retrieval_evidence.method: public search result and directory rendering
retrieval_evidence.evidence: Directory advertised 43,957 B2B companies and 928,650+ validated case studies/customer stories; scope is software purchasing rather than self-learning organizations.

collections.name: Talented Learning LMS Case Study Directory
collections.scope: Vendor-published learning-system customer case studies categorized by solution, use case, client industry, vendor, functionality, and services.
collections.raw_count_method: publisher-stated lower bound ('over 100'); stored numeric value is the advertised floor
collections.strict_count_method: Directory-scope audit only. Employee/member training-system adoption is not evidence that an organization has a self-improving operating loop.

retrieval_evidence.method: public search result and directory rendering
retrieval_evidence.evidence: Page says it assembled and categorized over 100 case studies from learning-system vendors and exposes vendor-oriented filters.

collections.name: ai-native-company-research
collections.scope: Public research notes on AI-native company operating systems, context engineering, internal agents, and self-improving loops.
collections.raw_count_unit: versioned content files excluding repository administration files
collections.raw_count_method: GitHub recursive tree: 24 blobs minus .gitignore, policy/contribution/license, and README = 19 content files
collections.strict_count_method: Complete filename/tree audit. The repository packages patterns, analyses, blueprints and source notes rather than one independently countable record per evidenced organization.
exclusions: source notes not normalized into organization case records

retrieval_evidence.method: GitHub public repository metadata and recursive tree API
retrieval_evidence.evidence: Repository default branch main had 24 blobs and was updated 2026-07-09T04:43:33Z; content paths comprise analyses, one blueprint, notes, patterns, and four source-note files.
